# Test basic mcp-shadow functionality

# Make echo.sh executable
exec chmod +x $WORK/echo.sh

# Use echo.sh as servers
exec sh -c 'echo "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"method\":\"test\",\"id\":1}" | mcp-shadow -primary "$WORK/echo.sh" -shadow "$WORK/echo.sh" -o shadow.mcp -q'

# Give it time to process
exec sleep 0.5

# Check that the trace file was created
exec test -f shadow.mcp
! stderr .

# Test with trace context
exec sh -c 'echo "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"method\":\"ping\",\"id\":1}" | mcp-shadow -primary "$WORK/echo.sh" -shadow "$WORK/echo.sh" -trace -baggage "test=true" -o traced.mcp -q'
exec sleep 0.5

# Check that trace file exists
exec test -f traced.mcp
! stderr .

-- echo.sh --
#!/bin/sh
read line
echo "$line"
